initial_program:
% Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 4connected neighbourhood
delta(1,0). delta(-1,0). delta(0,1). delta(0,-1).
neighbor(R1,C1,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC.

% Cells that lie on the outer border of the whole grid
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = N_rows-1.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = N_cols-1.

% Border of a layer: either on the outer border or adjacent to a strictly lower colour
border(R,C) :- edge(R,C).
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0,
               neighbor(R2,C2), input(R2,C2,N), N < V,
               R2 >= 0, R2 < N_rows, C2 >= 0, C2 < N_cols.

% Helper  every input position gets an output entry
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Output definition
% 1. Zero cells stay zero
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% 2. Cells that are on the border of their colour keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, border(R,C), Color = V.
% 3. Interior cells (no lower neighbour) become zero
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, not border(R,C), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

rounds.program_after:
% Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(N_rows) :- N_rows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N_cols) :- N_cols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 4connected neighbourhood
delta(1,0). delta(-1,0). delta(0,1). delta(0,-1).
neighbor(R1,C1,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC, R1 >= 0, R1 < N_rows, C1 >= 0, C1 < N_cols, R2 >= 0, R2 < N_rows, C2 >= 0, C2 < N_cols.

% Cells that lie on the outer border of the whole grid
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = N_rows-1.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = N_cols-1.

% Border of a layer: either on the outer border or adjacent to a strictly lower colour
border(R,C) :- edge(R,C).
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0,
               neighbor(R2,C2), input(R2,C2,N), N < V,
               R2 >= 0, R2 < N_rows, C2 >= 0, C2 < N_cols.

% Helper  every input position gets an output entry
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Output definition
% 1. Zero cells stay zero
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% 2. Cells that are on the border of their colour keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, border(R,C), Color = V.
% 3. Interior cells (no lower neighbour) become zero
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, not border(R,C), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

rounds.program_after:
% Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(N_rows) :- N_rows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N_cols) :- N_cols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 4connected neighbourhood
delta(1,0). delta(-1,0). delta(0,1). delta(0,-1).
neighbor(R1,C1,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R1 >= 0, R1 < N_rows, C1 >= 0, C1 < N_cols, R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC, R2 >= 0, R2 < N_rows, C2 >= 0, C2 < N_cols.

% Cells that lie on the outer border of the whole grid
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = N_rows-1.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = N_cols-1.

% Border of a layer: either on the outer border or adjacent to a strictly lower colour
border(R,C) :- edge(R,C), input(R,C,V), V > 0, neighbor(R2,C2), input(R2,C2,N), N < V, R2 >= 0, R2 < N_rows, C2 >= 0, C2 < N_cols.
% Helper  every input position gets an output entry
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Output definition
% 1. Zero cells stay zero
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% 2. Cells that are on the border of their colour keep their original colour

% Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(N_rows) :- N_rows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N_cols) :- N_cols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 4connected neighbourhood
delta(1,0). delta(-1,0). delta(0,1). delta(0,-1).
neighbor(R1,C1,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R1 >= 0, R1 < N_rows, C1 >= 0, C1 < N_cols, R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC, R2 >= 0, R2 < N_rows, C2 >= 0, C2 < N_cols.

% Cells that lie on the outer border of the whole grid
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = N_rows-1.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = N_cols-1.

% Border of a layer: either on the outer border or adjacent to a strictly lower colour
border(R,C) :- edge(R,C), input(R,C,V), V > 0, neighbor(R2,C2), input(R2,C2,N), N < V, R2 >= 0, R2 < N_rows, C2 >= 0, C2 < N_cols.
% Helper  every input position gets an output entry
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Output definition
% 1. Zero cells stay zero
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% 2. Cells that are on the border of their colour keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, border(R,C), Color = V.
% 3. Interior cells (no lower neighbour) become zero
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, not border(R,C), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).
grid(N_rows,N_cols) :- N_rows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}, N_cols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

delta(1,0). delta(-1,0). delta(0,1). delta(0,-1).
neighbor(R1,C1,R2,C2) :- delta(DR,DC), R1 >= 0, R1 < N_rows, C1 >= 0, C1 < N_cols, R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC, R2 >= 0, R2 < N_rows, C2 >= 0, C2 < N_cols.

% Cells that lie on the outer border of the whole grid
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, R = N_rows-1.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = 0.
edge(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, C = N_cols-1.

% Border of a layer: either on the outer border or adjacent to a strictly lower colour
border(R,C) :- edge(R,C), input(R,C,V), V > 0, neighbor(R2,C2), input(R2,C2,N), N < V, R2 >= 0, R2 < N_rows, C2 >= 0, C2 < N_cols.
% Helper  every input position gets an output entry
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Output definition
% 1. Zero cells stay zero
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% 2. Cells that are on the border of their colour keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, border(R,C), Color = V.
% 3. Interior cells (no lower neighbour) become zero
output(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), V > 0, not border(R,C), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).
grid(N_rows,N_cols) :- N_rows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}, N_cols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
